_meta.description: County-level permit information for unincorporated areas. Contractors working outside city limits must use county permit offices. This is a critical gap — many suburban contractors work in unincorporated areas.
_meta.importance: HIGH — a contractor in Katy TX uses Harris County (not Houston); Chandler AZ uses Maricopa County (not Phoenix); Lakewood CO uses Jefferson County (not Denver)

harris_county_tx.note: IMPORTANT: Unincorporated Harris County does NOT require residential permits for HVAC, electrical, or plumbing in most cases. Only flood plain and structural work needs county permits. Cities within Harris County (Houston, Pasadena, etc.) have their own permit requirements.

fees.residential_hvac: NOT REQUIRED in unincorporated areas for replacement work
fees.fee_note: Harris County unincorporated: HVAC replacement, electrical panel upgrade, water heater replacement do NOT require county permits. Check if address is within city limits first.

key_notes: Always verify whether address is in city limits or unincorporated county first
Cities like Katy, Spring, Humble are in Harris County but have own permit rules
Flood plain work always requires Harris County Flood Control permit
Commercial work requires permits regardless of incorporation status

maricopa_county_az.note: Maricopa County handles unincorporated areas. Incorporated cities (Phoenix, Scottsdale, Tempe, Chandler, Mesa, Gilbert, Glendale) have their own permit offices.

fees.hvac_mechanical: $85 minimum (unincorporated Maricopa County, 2025)
fees.fee_note: ROC (Registrar of Contractors) license required for all work in AZ

key_notes: ROC license number required on all permit applications in AZ
Maricopa County has some of the strictest wind/seismic requirements in AZ
Scottsdale, Tempe, Chandler, Mesa all have their own permit offices despite being in Maricopa County
Cave Creek, Queen Creek, Fountain Hills use Maricopa County permits
